title: Segfault in table with generated columns
description:
mrigger added on 2019-10-27 21:29:10:
Consider the following test case:
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INTEGER PRIMARY KEY GENERATED ALWAYS AS(1), c1 UNIQUE GENERATED ALWAYS AS(1), c2 UNIQUE);
INSERT INTO t0 VALUES(NULL); -- Segmentation fault
Unexpectedly, the INSERT results in a segfault. This is not a security issue, since generated columns are not yet available in a release version.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(c0 Int32 DEFAULT 1, c1 String DEFAULT 'default', c2 Float64) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY c0;
INSERT INTO t1 VALUES(NULL, NULL, NULL);
CREATE TABLE t2(c0 Int32, c1 Int32, c2 Int32 DEFAULT (c1 + c0)) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY c0;
INSERT INTO t2(c0, c1) VALUES(1, NULL);
CREATE TABLE t3(c0 Int32, c1 Int32, c2 Int32 DEFAULT (NULLIF(c1, 0))) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY c0;
key_bug_features: INSERT into table with generated columns causes segmentation fault
